summary: Frontend testing of REEFSIDE game - all critical screens tested: Title, Guardian Select, Game Canvas, HUD, Pause. Core flow works end-to-end.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal does not close with Escape key. No keyboard handler for Escape in the HowToPlay component. Pressing Escape leaves modal open and blocks title screen buttons (htp-overlay intercepts pointer events).
Button labeled 'UNDERSTOOD — DIVE IN' is misleading - it only closes the modal and returns to title screen, it does NOT start the game. User may expect to be taken directly to Guardian Select.
Browser tab title still says 'Aqua Quest - 8-Bit Underwater Adventure' instead of REEFSIDE

issues: Guardian card text is very small and hard to read in the ability descriptions (tiny pixel font on small cards)

action_items: Add Escape key handler to HowToPlay modal to close it (currently only closes by clicking overlay background or UNDERSTOOD button)
Update page title in index.html or App.js from 'Aqua Quest' to 'REEFSIDE'
Consider renaming 'UNDERSTOOD — DIVE IN' button to 'CLOSE' or 'GOT IT' since it returns to title, not the game
critical_code_review_comments: UnderwaterGame.jsx line 24: htp-overlay onClick closes modal but Escape key is not handled - user can get stuck if they don't click outside
index.html likely has stale page title 'Aqua Quest' - needs updating to REEFSIDE
context_for_next_testing_agent: All major game screens working: Title (REEFSIDE logo + BEGIN MISSION + HOW TO PLAY), Guardian Select (4 guardians with pixel art, SELECTED badge, DIVE IN button), Game Canvas (HUD with REEFSIDE, year 2025, 10-zone minimap, support meter, oxygen bar, player diver visible, pause with P key). Only issue is HOW TO PLAY modal doesn't close with Escape key - it only closes by clicking the overlay background or the UNDERSTOOD button.
